docs.title: Lesson overview
docs.text:
Description This course is composed of 7 sessions, based on the FAIR training handbook and 10 simple rules to make material FAIR publication. All sessions are structured in a way that complement each other aiming to introduce participants with a theoretical & hands-on approach of how to create FAIR material by design. Activities are thought to increase the technical skills and critical thinking during the development of a new training course. From A to Z participants are going to be challenged to work in a group to build a FAIR lesson on a topic to be presented at the end of the course. The topic will be disclaimed during the sessions, come inspired!

Prerequisites To be able to follow this course, learners should have knowledge in:  1. Git & GitHub usage (Make commits, create branches, open issues, make pull request)

Learning Outcomes: By the end of the course, learners will be able to:  1. Plan and prepare documentation about the training to increase reusability (Analyzing & Creating)  2. Use licenses to share your material and awknoledge others (Applying)  3. Differentiate types of training material and their FAIRness levels (Understanding & Evaluating)  4. Contrast and use GitHub and DOI to create FAIR releases of training material (Understanding & Applying)  5. To annotate and share the training material metadata (Applying)

Target Audience: PhD students, postdocs, researchers and anyone who wants to give training or is related to teaching .

Level: Beginner

License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License

Funding: This project has received funding from ELIXIR Europe.

docs.title: Citing this lesson
docs.text:
Please cite as:

  1. Bruna Piereck, Jill Jakowski, Nina Norgren, Elin Kronander, Alexander Botzki, Jessica Lindvall (2024). The ELIXIR FAIR training material by Design course
  2. Geert van Geest, Elin Kronander, Jose Alejandro Romero Herrera, Nadja Žlender, & Alexia Cardona. (2023). The ELIXIR Training Lesson Template - Developing Training Together (v1.0.0-alpha). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7913092.

docs.title: Data setup
docs.text: To run this lesson you need to clone the content of this github repository.

docs.title: Software setup
docs.text: To run this course you need to install an text editor of choice.

docs.title: DAY 1
docs.text: start end topic 09:30 10:15 What is FAIR and why do I care? 10:15 10:30 coffee! 10:30 12:30 Sylabus and documentation 12:30 13:15 Lunch ! 13:15 15:15 Train material formats and interoperability 15:50 15:30 coffee! 15:30 17:00 Ilustrations, aesthetics and acknoledgements

docs.text:
start end topic 09:30 11:30 Making e-learning FAIR, challanges and possibilities 11:30 11:45 coffee! 11:45 12:30 Releases and use of unique identifiers 12:30 13:15 Lunch ! 13:15 14:30 Releases and use of unique identifiers 14:30 14:45 coffee! 14:45 17:00 Course metadata, standardization and structure

docs.title: 1.1 First subtopic
docs.text:
Here you can enter text and create inline citations1 by using the bibtex plugin. Add your references in references.bib, and cite 2 by adding the @refid inside brackets like this [@10.1093/bioinformatics/btt113]

You can also embed videos from a local source (with a relative path) or from an url (like youtube). To use a youtube URL, just attach the ID of the video to a youtube embedded video link: https://youtube.com/embed/. For example, the Elixir training video https://youtu.be/oAD8FdGf8tI has the ID oAD8FdGf8tI, so the final link would be:

![type:video](https://youtube.com/embed/oAD8FdGf8tI)
